items.title: AI technologies could have saved ‘thousands of lives’ during covid, expert says
items.description: The health of humans, animals, and the entire ecosystem is inextricably linked. This message is at the heart of the One Health Summit, currently underway in France, where global health leaders are gathering. The summit aims to explore future strategies, particularly using emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, to ensure – and even improve – the delicate balance between these elements. We spoke to Antoine Tesnière, who advised the French government during the Covid pandemic, about how new technologies could have saved lives had they been available at the time.

items.title: News24 | ‘There will be nothing like it’: Trump threatens 'hell' for Iran over Strait of Hormuz
items.description: US President Donald Trump threatened in an expletive-laden post on Sunday to strike Iran’s power plants and bridges if it does not reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz, after announcing the rescue of an airman in a “miraculous” operation.

items.title: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri bring “Proof” back to broadway
items.description: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri make their Broadway debuts in a new revival of “Proof,” David Auburn’s Tony Award and Pulitzer Prize-winning play. Edebiri, the Emmy-winning actor from “The Bear,” admits the experience has not quite “sunk in.”

items.title: Congo to take third-country deportees from the US under new deal

items.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
items.description: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

items.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
items.description: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

items.title: Watch: Israel confirms U.S.-Israeli strikes kill Iran Guards intelligence chief
items.description: Israel’s Defence Minister says Iran’s Revolutionary Guards Corps intelligence chief Majid Khademi has been killed in a strike in Tehran. Iran calls it a “terrorist attack” and blames Israel and the United States. The killing marks the latest in a series of strikes targeting top Iranian leadership since late February.

items.title: News24 | How long will it take Artemis to reach the moon?
items.description: NASA has successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed mission to the moon’s vicinity since the Apollo programme ended in 1972.

items.title: As Iran war rages, Europe gears up for energy crisis

items.title: Trump says Iran could be 'taken out' on Tuesday

items.title: Germany's Merz sees backlash over Syrian refugee comment

items.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
